english: śo-rgyán (sho rgyan) Cs. the money or stake deposited at dice-playing; rtsís-śòi (rtsis sho'i) rdeu (rde'u) Mil., śo-rdél (sho rdel) Wdk., an attribute of certain deities; śo-míg (sho mig) the points of dice, śo-mig (sho mig) γsúm-par (gsum par) (or -pa-la) (-pa la) °báb-na ('bab na) when three points are thrown. Tibetans play with three dice marked with 6 and 1, 5 and 4, 3 and 2 on opposite sides, hence from 3 to 18 points may be thrown. II. 1. the white willow of Spiti, Ld. and other Himalayan districts. — 2. other plants rgya-śò (rgya sho), lug-śò? (lug sho?) Wdn. III. = btsa-ma (btsa ma) blast, blight, smut, mildew Cs. IV. for śo-gam (sho gam), q. v. V. num.: 147.

custom, duty, tax, śo-gám (sho gam) lći-ba (lci ba) W. *lćin-te* (*lcin te*) high duty, śo-gam (sho gam) len-pa (len pa) to take toll, to levy a duty; śo-gám (sho gam) bzlá-ba (bzla ba) Sch.: 'to smuggle, to circumvent or defraud the customs' (?); śo-gám-gyi (sho gam gyi) γnas (gnas) custom-house; śo-gám-pa (sho gam pa) receiver of the customs, toll-gatherer; *śo-ṭál* (*sho ṭal*), *śo-dú* (*sho du*) Ts. tax, duty.
saltpetre, nitre, śó-ra-ćan (sho ra can) nitrous.

adj., damaged, spoiled, by being partially broken, torn etc., sbst.: a defect, flaw, notch, gap, also hare-lip; *śo-ré soṅ* (*sho re song*) it is damaged, *śo-ré ton soṅ* (*sho re ton song*) a notch, chink, crack has been caused; *k'a-śór* (*kha shor*), *na-śór* (*na shor*), with a slit lip, a slit nose.

I. śóg-ćig (shog cig), prop. from γśégs-pa (gshegs pa), imp. of °oṅ-ba ('ong ba), 1. come! let him come! °brás-bu ('bras bu) tsúr-śog-gi (tsur shog gi) yid-smón (yid smon) ṅá-la (nga la) med (med) I do not wish that fruit should come to me from without Mil.; k'ur (khur) śog (shog), k'yer (khyer) śog (shog) bskyal (bskyal) śog (shog) bring hither, (with soṅ (song) inst. of śog (shog): take away!) śog (shog) zér-ba (zer ba) to invite. ṅed-la (nged la) śog (shog) kyaṅ (kyang) mi (mi) zer (zer) Glr. we are not so much as invited, you know. — 2. with the imp. = gyur-ćig (gyur cig), bsad-par-śog (bsad par shog) may (he, I etc.) be killed! Dzl. — II. v. śos (shos).

1. sbst., also frq. γśóg-pa (gshog pa), 1. wing, γśóg-rkyaṅ-ba (gshog rkyang ba) to spread the wings, also to spread like wings; *śóg-pa ḍé-će* (*shog pa de ce*), *ćád-će* (*cad ce*), *ḍúm-će* (*dum ce*) W. to clip the wings; γśóg-pa-ćan (gshog pa can), γśog-ldán (gshog ldan) provided with wings, winged, a bird. — 2. wing-feather, pinion, °dab-γśóg ('dab gshog), γśóg-sgro (gshog sgro) id.; mjug-γśog (mjug gshog) tail-feather. — 3. fin, of fishes. — 4. other things resembling a wing or a feather, mig-γśog (mig gshog), resp. spyán-γśog (spyan gshog) eye-lash; raṅ-t'ág-gi (rang thag gi) γśóg-pa (gshog pa) prob.: wing or float-board of a water-mill; of course it might also be used for: wing, sail, of a windmill, though these are not yet known in Tibet. II. vb., v. sub γśog-pa (gshog pa).

W. *śúg-gu* (*shug gu*), 1. sheet of paper, and paper collectively, rgya-śóg (rgya shog) China paper, bod-śóg (bod shog) Tibet paper, dar-śóg (dar shog) silk-paper, ras-śóg (ras shog) cotton-paper (also paper of linen-rags), śiṅ-śóg (shing shog) bast-paper, pags-śóg (pags shog) leather-paper, skin-paper, parchment; mt'iṅ-śóg (mthing shog), nag-śóg (nag shog) dark-blue or black paper, for writing on in gold or silver; mgo-śóg (mgo shog), resp. dbu-śóg (dbu shog), upper leaf, i.e. cover, covering, wrapper. — 2. Bal.: book. Comp. śog-k'áṅ (shog khang) paper-maker's form. — śog-gáṅ (shog gang) a sheet of paper. — śog-gráṅs (shog grangs) number of leaves in a book. — śog-sgríl (shog sgril), śog-dríl (shog dril), W. *śog-ril* (*shog ril*) paper-roll, codex. — śog-ldéb (shog ldeb) Sch.: 'leaf, sheet'? — śog-tsár (shog tsar) scrap of paper. — śog-śiṅ (shog shing) Sch. palm-tree? — śog-hríl (shog hril) γćig (gcig) = śog-gáṅ (shog gang), Sch.

(Lex.: 'elevated plain, ridge of a mountain') 1. mountain-ridge Wts. Usual meaning: 2. pit, hole, cavity, excavation, valley, ć'u-śoṅs (chu shongs) cavity filled with water Dzl.; spaṅ-śoṅs (spang shongs) valley with meadows, low ground overgrown with grass; snai (sna'i) bya-γśóg-gi (bya gshog gi) γśoṅs (gshongs) the cavities near the wings of the nose Mil. nt.; śoṅ-du (shong du) valley-ward, down hill Dzl.; śoṅs-ćan (shongs can), (γ)śoṅ-(γ)śoṅ ((g)shong (g)shong) full of cavities, uneven, Sch. also: rough, rugged, steep; śoṅs-bu (shongs bu) furrow, śoṅs-bu (shongs bu) °tén-pa ('ten pa) to make furrows, to furrow.
I. to go in, to have room in or on, with term., mi (mi) śoṅ (shong), W. also: *śoṅ-će* (*shong ce*) *mi* (*mi*) *dug* (*dug*) that is not to be got in,
